h1. GraphRAG Orchestration

h2. Purpose

Combine exact lookup, semantic search, graph traversal, reranking, and answer composition into a grounded AI answer flow.

h2. Retrieval Strategies

|| Strategy || Use Case ||
| Exact-first | User gives table, JRN, GUID, report ID |
| Semantic-first | User asks business-language discovery question |
| Graph-first | User asks upstream/downstream/impact question |
| Hybrid | User asks broad question requiring multiple retrieval modes |

h2. Example Flow: Downstream Impact

{code}
User asks: What downstream reports depend on customer_transactions?

1. Resolve customer_transactions to canonical entity/JRN.
2. Use Neo4j to traverse downstream relationships.
3. Fetch related source records from MongoDB.
4. Retrieve relevant AI chunks.
5. Assemble context.
6. Generate answer with source references.
{code}

h2. Requirements

|| ID || Requirement || Priority ||
| RAG-001 | Classify question intent | P0 |
| RAG-002 | Resolve mentioned entities | P0 |
| RAG-003 | Choose retrieval strategy | P0 |
| RAG-004 | Expand graph relationships | P1 |
| RAG-005 | Assemble bounded context | P1 |
| RAG-006 | Generate grounded answers | P1 |
| RAG-007 | Return source references | P1 |
| RAG-008 | Ask clarification when ambiguous | P1 |

h2. Success Criteria

* GraphRAG chooses the right retrieval path.
* Ambiguous entities trigger clarification.
* Answers are source-backed.
* Missing metadata is stated clearly.
* No unsupported metadata claims are generated.

h2. Pitfalls / Risks

|| Risk || Impact || Mitigation ||
| Ambiguous entity resolution | Wrong answer | Clarify before answering |
| Too much context | Slow/low-quality LLM answers | Bounded context assembly |
| Unsupported claims | Loss of trust | Answer only from retrieved source context |
| Graph-only answers miss semantic context | Incomplete answer | Combine graph traversal with chunk retrieval |
